heroHeadline: Sheinbaum at 565 Days: Teotihuacán Reopens with National Guard Surge; Ingrid Gómez Named New Secretary of Women
heroSubtitle: Teotihuacán's archaeological zone reopened April 22 at 8 AM with a National Guard surge two days after a mass shooting killed a Canadian tourist — but the Pyramid of the Moon remains closed. Security Secretary García Harfuch announced 100,000 security forces deployed across Mexico's three FIFA World Cup 2026 host cities and evaluation of metal detectors at heritage sites. At the same mañanera, Sheinbaum formally presented Ingrid Gómez Saracibar — her former CDMX gender violence chief — as the new Secretary of Women, replacing Citlalli Hernández who left to lead Morena's National Elections Commission. The peso held steady at ~17.32 MXN per USD, mid-range between its year high of 18.16 and year low of 17.09, with USMCA Round 2 mechanics continuing to provide support ahead of the first formal negotiating session scheduled for May 25.
footerNote: Data sourced from Diario Oficial, INEGI, Banco de México, SNSP, Secretaría de Economía, INAH, Reuters, AP, El Universal, Reforma, Animal Político, Mexico News Daily, Infobae, Milenio, La Jornada, Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, AtlasIntel, USTR, Chatham House, Mexico Business News, El Financiero, La Silla Rota, Proceso, CNN Español, Lopez Doriga, The Yucatan Times, Excelsior, La Razón de México, Quinto Poder, PBS NewsHour, NBC News, Time, NPR, CBC, Japan Kantei, Japan MOFA, SDP Noticias
